title: After Hours Update
commentary: Markets wrapped Wednesday's session in mixed fashion as tech led gains while commodities cooled. Fed policy signals continue to anchor expectations with traders pricing steady rates through mid-year. Gold retreated from recent highs on dollar strength while crude oil consolidated near $75 amid OPEC+ production uncertainty.

title: Close: Precious Metals Bloodbath Caps Brutal Week
commentary: Gold crashes 6.4% to $4,585 in worst single-day rout since 2013 as margin liquidations cascade—silver implodes nearly 10% amid flight from leveraged commodity longs. Oil holds at $100.56 (+4.4%) as Iran conflict intensifies; WTI briefly tagged $119 intraday after Qatar LNG facility strikes. Equities end red with Dow -0.7%, S&P -0.5% as Fed signals no 2026 cuts. Crypto bleeds alongside risk: BTC -2.8% at $69,414, Extreme Fear at 23.

title: Late Session: Selloff Deepens, BTC Breaks $70K Support
commentary: Equities extend losses into late session with Dow -0.8%, S&P -0.6% as oil spike to $97 keeps pressure on risk assets—Micron's post-earnings plunge adds to tech malaise. Precious metals carnage continues: gold at $4,597 (-2.8%), silver in freefall at $69.90 (-13%) as margin liquidation cascades. Bitcoin breaks critical $70K support, trading at $69,453 as Iran conflict escalation and fading rate-cut hopes hammer crypto sentiment.

title: Midday Meltdown: Precious Metals Crater on Margin Calls
commentary: Gold plunges nearly 7% to $4,561 in worst single-day drop since 2013 as margin liquidations cascade through commodity markets—silver follows with 12.4% crash to $68. Equities extend post-Fed losses with Dow -0.5%, S&P -0.6% as oil volatility and Powell's inflation warnings keep buyers sidelined. Dollar weakens to 99.7 as safe-haven flows shift to cash. BTC outperforms risk assets at $69,940, holding above key support while TradFi deleverages.

title: US Open: Oil Spikes to $119, Market Rout Extends
commentary: Brent briefly breaches $119 after Iranian strikes hit Qatar LNG facilities—commodity strategists revive $150 oil warnings as energy war escalates. Dow futures tumble 300 points extending post-Fed carnage; S&P 6,625 marks fifth straight session of losses. BTC drops to $69,270 (-2.8%) but outperforms equities as gold/silver sell-off accelerates on margin liquidation. Micron plunges 5% despite beat—AI capex concerns overshadow blockbuster guide.

title: Asia Open: Dow Hits 2026 Low, Asia Shrugs It Off
commentary: Wall Street caps brutal Fed Day with Dow crashing 771 points to new 2026 low—inflation fears eclipse Fed's rate hold as Powell flags 'elevated uncertainty.' Oil rips back to $108 on Iran escalation; crypto carnage continues with BTC -4.6%, ETH -5.9%. Asia opens green despite the bloodbath: Nikkei surges 2.9%, Hang Seng +0.6% as investors bet Powell's dovish undertones outweigh near-term pain.

title: After-Hours: Powell Press Conference Fallout
commentary: Markets extend losses in after-hours as Powell acknowledges 'economic shocks' from Iran conflict—Dow ends down 600 points, S&P -0.6%. Crypto dumps accelerate with BTC -4.9% at $71,026, ETH cratering -6.5% to $2,179 as risk-off deepens. Asia opens green defying Wall St. weakness: Nikkei +2.9%, Hang Seng +0.6%. Oil retreats from $104 highs but Iran premium persists.

title: Post-Close: Fed Holds, Markets Digest
commentary: Stocks close near session lows with Dow -0.9%, Nasdaq -0.5% as hot PPI data overshadows Fed hold. Oil breaches $104—four-year highs—on Iran escalation and Strait of Hormuz fears. Crypto dumps hard: BTC -3.1% to $71,639, ETH sub-$2,205 as risk-off contagion spreads. Asia futures green: Nikkei +2.9%, HSI +0.6% shrug off Wall St. weakness for now.

title: Fed Day Close: Risk Rout Deepens Pre-Decision
commentary: Wall Street bleeds into Fed decision with Dow -1.0%, S&P -0.6% as hot PPI data and Iran oil fears collide. Gold crashes -2.8% to $4,867 in brutal deleveraging while silver plunges -3.9%. Oil holds gains near $98 (+2.1%) as Trump's Jones Act waiver fails to cool supply fears. Crypto carnage continues—BTC -3.3%, ETH -5.6%—as risk assets face dual headwinds of sticky inflation and geopolitical turmoil.

title: Fed Day Afternoon: Oil Tests $100, Risk Dumps
commentary: WTI crude flirts with $100 (+3.2%) after Iran reports attacks on key oil and gas fields—'black swan' moment for energy as strategists warn of 20% equity downside risk. Precious metals hammered in flight from leveraged longs: gold -2.8% to $4,869, silver crashes -4.2%. Equities extend losses with Dow -0.8% as Coca-Cola and P&G drag. Crypto capitulates alongside risk assets, BTC plunging 4.7% below $72K.
